date line--"The generally accepted
opinion Is that Stevenson is endeavor-
ing to break into the ranks Of the
Whitman county delegation for votes
against the railroad commission bill.
'The effort of Stevenson to
break into Whitman county is sur
prising to politicians here because Of
the strong platform which the Whit
man county republicans adopted on


the commission question.
The attempt of Stevenson to secure
votes In Whitman county is looked
upon as an indication that the claim
of the railroads that they control the
senate is hot air and that they are
really frightened over the prospect of
a break in King county,"


December "a, 1902, under Seattle
date line-"But the Ankeny has been
managed with such consummate skill
and astuteness by George Stevenson,
the o. r. & N. lobbyist"


January 2, 1908, in local columns
The governors fight has apparently
| accomplished one thing already 1t
Is given out that George ii. Stevenson


the big chief of the railroad lobby,


has decided not to go to Olympia at
ALL"


January 16, 1903.-Local columns
make reference to the fact that in the
city are railroad lobbyists of high
and low degree" the names of George
